response.justification: Wei Chen is fundamentally a full-stack web/TypeScript engineer with a broad but shallow systems background. While there is genuine geospatial work via GISCollective and early C++ exposure at National Instruments, the C++ experience is a decade old and limited to IDE component writing — far from the production-grade routing pipeline systems engineering this role demands. No Rust experience is mentioned, no routing tile or navigation data pipeline background exists, and the candidate's current trajectory is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js and AI tooling, making this a poor fit for a geospatial C++ systems role.
strengths: Demonstrated geospatial platform development through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data, GIS application, FOSS4G talk)
Distributed systems and high-throughput service experience at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Broad Linux and DevOps familiarity across multiple roles, including container orchestration and CI infrastructure
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (2014–2015 at National Instruments); not listed in skills section at all
No Rust experience and no modern systems language proficiency relevant to the role
Zero experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, or traffic signal integration
